History Exam — Colonial Nigeria
SSS2 | WAEC Preparation

This exam covers the colonial period of Nigerian history.

The student should know about British colonisation. Define the term 'colonialism'. List three reasons why Britain colonised Nigeria. Name the first Governor-General. State the date of Nigerian independence.

Remember to write neatly. You have 30 minutes to complete this test.

Students who finish early can recall additional details about the colonial economy.

Mark scheme: 2 marks per correct definition, 1 mark per reason listed.
reference: WAEC-style SSS2 History exam on Colonial Nigeria requiring students to evaluate and critique the impact of colonisation using evidence — EVALUATE level cognition.
subject: history
notes: Three failures: (1) EVALUATE expected but uses REMEMBER verbs (define, list, name, state, recall, know, remember) — no evaluate/justify/assess/critique verbs. (2) No question numbers (Q1, Q2...) — format_compliance fails. (3) Missing WAEC structural markers.
